content:
Ontario’s biggest grants are designed to incentivize job creation outside the Greater Toronto Area. If you want a massive expansion grant, you will have vastly better odds targeting a facility in Southwestern Ontario (SWODF), Eastern Ontario (EODF), or Northern Ontario (NOHFC) rather than competing with ten thousand startups in downtown Toronto.

The Golden Key: Your Local "SBEC"

❓ Common Questions About Ontario Grants

How do I get the $5,000 Starter Company grant?

Requirements for the Small Business Enterprise Centre program.

What is the SWODF/EODF fund?

Explaining the major regional expansion funds.

Is the Digital Main Street grant open?

Current status of the $2,500 digital transformation grant.

Where can tech startups get funding?

Guide to MaRS, Communitech, and Regional Innovation Centres.

Are there grants for hiring via Employment Ontario?

subsidies for apprentices and youth employment.

Who is eligible for Summer Company?

Student grant details and deadlines.

What is Starter Company Plus?

The Deal

  • 18+ Ontario Residents
  • Not in school full-time
  • Launching OR Expanding
  • 3-6 months mentorship
  • Write a full Business Plan
  • "Pitch" for the money

Comparison: Summer Company

What are the "Acronym" Funds (SWODF, EODF, NOHFC)?

Where the BIG money lives ($100k+)

SWODF

EODF

(Ottawa, Kingston, Peterborough)

NOHFC

Generous conditional contributions

What is the "Golden Horseshoe" Myth?

1. Mastering SWODF / EODF

  • The "Job Creation" Math: The province calculates the "ROI" of your grant based on tax revenue from new employees. If you ask for $100k but only plan to hire 1 person at minimum wage, you will be rejected. You generally need to show 5-10 net new high-paying jobs to unlock the six-figure grants.
  • Stacking Rule: You can stack this with federal loans (like BDC), but usually not with other provincial grants.

2. Youth Entrepreneurship: Summer Company

3. Digital Main Street (DMS) 4.0

4. Futurepreneur Ontario

What is Digital Main Street (ShopHERE)?

While the famous $2,500 grant comes and goes, the ShopHERE program is a constant gem. It builds your e-commerce store for free.

What you get

Students build it for you. You just provide the photos and text. This saves you 40+ hours of work.

They don't just hand it over; they teach you how to manage inventory and shipping.

What are Regional Innovation Centres (RICs)?

If you are a tech startup, stop looking at "SBECs" and start looking at "RICs". Ontario funds these hubs to scale tech.

The behemoth. Specialized streams for Health, Cleantech, and Fintech. Hard to get into, but massive investor access.

For deep tech and SaaS. Known for connecting startups with big corporate buyers.

Focus on autonomous vehicles and defense via Area X.O.

Great for energy and smart city startups.

What is Manufacturing (AMIC)?

What it funds:

Eligibility

Other Provincial Business Programs

Related Funding Guides

How Do You Find Your Local SBEC?
shortAnswer: Yes — Ontario small businesses can access regional capital support (RDP matching grants), workforce training credits (COJG matching up to 83% / $15,000 per trainee), and municipal Starter Company Plus grants ($5,000). These programs stack with federal resources like IRAP and SR&ED to cover a substantial portion of R&D costs.
shortAnswerQuestion: What small business grants and funding are available in Ontario for 2026?

faq.question: What is the Canada-Ontario Job Grant (COJG) limit?
faq.answer: COJG covers up to 50% to 83% of eligible workforce training fees (such as course tuition and books), capped at a maximum of $15,000 per trainee.

faq.question: Are Starter Company Plus grants repayable?
faq.answer: No. Starter Company Plus grants of up to $5,000 are non-repayable, but require completion of a structured business training curriculum and business plan review.

faq.question: What is the proposed corporate tax rate change for Ontario small businesses?
faq.answer: The 2026 Ontario Budget proposed reducing the small business corporate tax rate from 3.2% to 2.2% (effective July 1, 2026) for qualifying CCPCs on active business income up to $500,000.

expertTip.title: Apply Through Your Local SBEC
expertTip.content: Starter Company Plus and local entrepreneurship support are managed directly by regional Small Business Enterprise Centres (SBECs). Book an introductory call with an advisor to review requirements before submitting.
